packages.title: Academic Board — Agenda Package · December 4, 2025
packages.summary: The Academic Board reviewed proposed new graduate programs, the annual report on academic appeals, and a planning report on enrolment, and received the Provost's report on the budget outlook.
packages.gradRelevance: New graduate programs and the budget outlook shape funding packages, supervision capacity and program offerings for grad students.

documents.title: Annual Report on Academic Appeals
documents.summary: Yearly summary of academic appeals heard across divisions and their outcomes.
documents.gradRelevance: Relevant to grad students navigating grade and academic-standing appeals.

packages.title: Audit Committee — Agenda Package · November 18, 2025
packages.summary: The committee received the external auditor's interim findings and an update on the enterprise risk management framework, and approved the internal audit workplan with a focus on cybersecurity and research grant compliance.
packages.gradRelevance: Research grant compliance and risk oversight touch the funding and labs many grad students work in.
keyItems: Internal audit workplan approved (cybersecurity, research grants)

packages.title: Business Board — Agenda Package · February 25, 2026
packages.summary: The Business Board reviewed the capital projects portfolio including the Spadina–Sussex residence, the second-quarter financial report, and a proposed schedule of ancillary fees for 2026–27.
packages.gradRelevance: Ancillary fees and residence projects directly affect what grad students pay and where they can live.

documents.title: Ancillary Fees Schedule 2026–27
documents.summary: Proposed ancillary fee rates for the coming year across services and facilities.
documents.gradRelevance: Sets fees grad students pay on top of tuition.

packages.title: Business Board — Agenda Package · July 9, 2026
packages.summary: Upcoming year-end Business Board meeting expected to approve the annual operating budget and review investment performance and the capital plan.
packages.gradRelevance: The operating budget approval determines the funding envelope grad students' departments and TA budgets draw on.

bodies.name: Committee on Academic Policy and Programs

packages.title: Governing Council — Agenda Package · April 30, 2026
packages.summary: The Governing Council received reports from its boards and committees and considered final approvals for the academic year, including the operating budget recommended through the Business and Academic Boards.
packages.gradRelevance: As the University's senior body, its budget and policy approvals set the context for all grad-student funding and services.

packages.title: Planning and Budget Committee — Agenda Package · January 21, 2026
packages.summary: The committee reviewed the long-range budget guidelines and a report on deferred maintenance before recommending the operating budget framework to the Business and Academic Boards.
packages.gradRelevance: Budget guidelines set the envelope for graduate funding, TA/RA budgets and program support.

packages.title: University Affairs Board — Agenda Package · October 23, 2025
packages.summary: At its Cycle 1 meeting the University Affairs Board worked through a heavily student-facing agenda: proposed revisions to the Policy on Sexual Violence and Sexual Harassment plus the annual report under it, a presentation on the Hart House Building Renewal, the Multi-Year Accessibility Plan, and a motion to approve in principle the MacMillan Theatre Remediation (~1,200 gsm, Provostial funds).
packages.gradRelevance: The SVSH policy revisions set the protections, supports and complaint process covering grad students, TAs and RAs; the accessibility plan determines accommodations for disabled grad students; and Hart House is funded partly by student fees.
keyItems: Proposed revisions to the SVSH Policy (for information)
Annual report on activity under the SVSH Policy
MacMillan Theatre Remediation approved in principle (~1,200 gsm, Provostial funds)

documents.title: Proposed Revisions: Policy on Sexual Violence and Sexual Harassment
documents.summary: Proposed amendments to the SVSH policy covering definitions, supports, and the disclosure/complaint process.
documents.gradRelevance: Directly governs protections and reporting options for grad students, TAs and RAs.

documents.title: Multi-Year Accessibility Plan (MYAP)
documents.summary: The University's multi-year plan for meeting accessibility obligations across facilities, services and academics.
documents.gradRelevance: Determines accommodations available to disabled grad students.

documents.title: Hart House Building Renewal (Presentation)
documents.summary: Update on the planned renewal of the Hart House building and its spaces.
documents.gradRelevance: Hart House is funded partly by student fees and widely used by grad students.

bodies.name: Ad Hoc Committee for Capital Projects
